header.title: Jin Idol Protection
header.subtitle: Construction work for leakage and other issues in Jain temples

intro.p1: A beautiful idol has the power to grant right faith (Samkit) to thousands. If due to our ignorance the idol gets damaged and instead of devotional joy clouds of worry arise in the mind,
intro.p1Highlight: what a great loss it is!
intro.p2: While researching its root causes, Adhyatma Parivar even made efforts to knock on the doors of

problems.title: Is this a picture of the idol or a picture of our ignorance?

items.title: Bitter Reality
items.desc: The bitter reality revealed after removing the glued layers!

items.title: The Miserable Condition of the Lord’s Feet
items.desc:
Where golden lotuses were once placed at the Lord’s feet,
 the painful condition caused by glued patches!

items.title: Cause of Deterioration
items.desc: The main reason for this deterioration is nothing but the moisture trapped inside tika and similar substances.

items.title: Protection by Dharanendra
items.desc:
Lord Parshwanath was protected by Dharanendra.
 But we could not protect the hood-like reflection of the Lord!

rootCause.title: Important Conclusion
rootCause.ancientWisdom: What ASI has stated was written centuries ago in Shri Shraddhavidhi Granth:
rootCause.quote: If even a little water remains on the idol, darkness (blackening) appears there.
rootCause.mainTitle: If we cut only the branches instead of the root of a babul tree, how will the thorns stop pricking?
steps: First, a 1-inch tika was applied, creating a cavity!
To cover it, a 1.5-inch tika was applied, creating another cavity!
To hide both, a 2-inch tika was applied, resulting in a third cavity!

story.title: A Milestone of Fortune: In the Courtyard of Antarikshji...

sections.title: The Period of Waiting
content: Who is unaware of the unspoken pain in the hearts of millions for 15,060 days?
When the presiding Lord of Shri Antarikshji was locked behind doors.
How much expenditure... how many obstacles...
How much penance... how many decades, months, and hours of long waiting...

sections.title: Fruit of Devotion
content: Finally, that devotion bore fruit in the form of a historic interim order from the Supreme Court.
Now the opportunity to worship Dada was about to be restored.
The coating work that had been halted for decades had to be completed.

sections.title: Preparation and Challenge
content: Meetings of Jain Sanghs across India began.
What will happen? How will it happen? Will it happen properly?
At that time, the fully dedicated Shasan servant of Adhyatma Parivar was contacted.
He was asked: Can you undertake the coating work of Antarikshji’s Dada?

sections.title: Dedication and Success
content: Every nerve of the dedicated Shasan servant of Adhyatma Parivar thrilled with devotion.
But upon arrival, the grave situation and life risk became evident.
Yet before the joy of devotion to Dada, fear of risk faded away.
With devotion to Dada and the auspicious blessings of the fourfold Sangh of India, the sacred coating work was successfully completed.

images.title: The trustees entrusted this critical task to Adhyatma Parivar because they knew that...
images.label1: Fraudulent craftsmen use cheap materials like M-seal and cause damage to the Lord!
images.label2: Adhyatma Parivar uses pure diamond powder and only pure materials in all coating and polishing work.

items.title: Shri Shatrunjaya Tirthadhipati Adinath Dada
items.desc: Since V.S. 2075-76, every year from Bhadarva Sud Chhath to Asoj Sud Chhath (one month), the purification and polishing work of Adinath Dada and other idols at the great pilgrimage site of Shatrunjaya has been continuously carried out.

items.title: Shri Girnar Mahatirth
items.desc: The coating of the world’s most ancient idol — Shri Neminath Dada at Girnar Mahatirth — with scripturally prescribed pure substances, and polishing of the manifest powerful Shri Amizara Parshwanath Parmatma, completed by Adhyatma Parivar!

items.title: Shri Samet Shikhar Mahatirth
items.desc: Coating work of Shri Shyamala Parshwanath Parmatma in the Jalmandir of Samet Shikhar Mahatirth, sanctified by the Moksha Kalyanak of 20 Tirthankars of this Avasarpini era.

items.title: Shri Shankheshwar Mahatirth
items.desc: Polishing work of all the deities enshrined in the temple premises.

items.title: Shri Makshiji Tirth
items.desc: Coating of Shri Makshiji Parshwanath Parmatma and polishing work of all idols in the 52 Jinalayas completed joyfully by Adhyatma Parivar!

pledge.title: One Resolution of Every Devotee to Prevent Future Damage
pledge.text1: 'I will dedicate 1 minute daily for the protection of the heritage of the Shasan.'
pledge.text2: I will dry my Lord with a clean cloth before performing worship.

cta.heading: What about the damage that has already occurred?
cta.content: Adhyatma Parivar has painstakingly researched pure substances to remove tika and perform proper coating and polishing of idols. Please allow and help us extend purification and protection to as many idols as possible.

jinpratimaAashatanaNivaran.metaTitle: Jin Idol Ashatana Prevention | Jinmurti Department

header.title: Jin Idol Ashatana Prevention
header.subtitle: Proper arrangement for idols without Anjan ritual

content.quote: In Shri Shodashak Granth Sho.8 Ga.1, Pujya Haribhadrasuri Maharaj states:
content.quoteText: 'Within ten days after making an idol, the Anjan ritual must be performed.'
content.desc: After hearing this from the Sadguru, a devoted soul becomes deeply concerned about what to do with stone, metal, or fiber idols that were previously accepted at home as 'gifts' without proper understanding.
content.solution: To ensure that such idols do not incur Ashatana, Adhyatma Parivar is prepared to arrange proper solutions.

cta.heading: Do you have such an idol that is not fit for worship?

jinpratimaNirmaanDhatu.metaTitle: Jin Idol Construction: Metal | Jin Idol Protection

intro.text: This is the second achievement of Adhyatma Shilpashala. In the construction of metal idols, a combination of modern technology and traditional craftsmanship is applied.

sections.title: Vacuum Investment Casting
sections.text: As previously described (in stone idol construction), a clay model is first prepared. Then a wax die is made, and through the vacuum investment casting method, the metal idol is created.

sections.title: Specialized Detailing
sections.text: Adhyatma Parivar is ready to provide beautifully detailed idols of the Lord, Siddhachakraji, and Parikar. Every artwork is crafted with careful attention to the finest details.

jinpratimaNirmaanPashaan.metaTitle: Jin Idol Construction - Stone | Jinmurti Department

intro.title: Today, for most idols, 'whiteness' has become the primary and only criterion for selecting stone. In reality, the order should be:

criteria.title: Porosity Test:
criteria.desc: Stone with high porosity absorbs more water, as a result of which the idol may deteriorate within a few years. Therefore, this is the first criterion.

criteria.title: Sulfur Content Consideration:
criteria.desc: In parts of the stone where sulfur content is high, even a slight impact can cause a fragment to break off from the idol.

criteria.title: Whiteness:
criteria.desc: After ensuring the above two factors, whiteness (spotless appearance) should be considered third for devotional appeal.

process.title: Porosity Testing Method
process.desc: A small hole is made in a piece of stone and stain is applied. The more the color spreads, the higher the porosity of the stone. Such stone is not used for idol construction.

process.title: Identification of Sulfur Lines
process.desc: The line visible here indicates sulfur presence. It is extremely risky, as it increases the chances of cracks or breakage in the idol.

process.title: Initial Model Creation
process.desc: According to scriptural measurements, an attractive clay model is first created. This model becomes the foundation for all further processes.

process.title: Digital Processing
process.desc: The clay model is 3D scanned and finely edited using computer technology to refine its form. Modern technology enhances traditional art further.

process.title: Initial Design and Proportion
process.desc: These beautiful idols are crafted with such precision that the measurements of left-right and even the smallest limbs and sub-parts reflect the symmetry of the Lord’s perfect proportions.

conclusion.text: The Adhyatma Shilpashala initiative started by Adhyatma Parivar in Himmatnagar, Gujarat, is a multidimensional project with extremely fascinating aspects.

jinpratimaSangrahan.metaTitle: Ancient Jin Idol Collection | Jin Idol Protection

intro.text: Idols discovered from the ground should not go to government museums or other inappropriate places, but instead be worshipped at suitable locations. Therefore, such idols are purified and, if required, coated, and then installed at proper places. This work is progressing smoothly.

highlight.text: Simultaneously, in deserted villages where idols may remain unworshipped, Adhyatma Parivar is acting as a 'bridge' to ensure they are worshipped at appropriate locations.

success.title: Success Stories
success.story1: The extremely ancient Adinath Dada found in a field near Deesa now shines as the presiding deity at Shri Shantikanak Jain Sangh, Nanpura-Surat Jinalaya.
success.story2: Here is the extremely ancient Dharmanath Dada, now enshrined as the main deity at the four Kalyanak land Ratnapuri (Ranohi) in Uttar Pradesh.
success.story2End: This idol was discovered from a field near Himmatnagar, Gujarat.

cta.heading: Do you have information about such idols?
cta.content: We can assist in purification and installation at an appropriate place.
